factors.summary: The company is subject to product-related litigation and regularly evaluates loss contingencies.

evidence.text: We are subject to the possibility of various loss contingencies arising in the ordinary course of business, including product-related litigation. We consider the likelihood of loss or impairment of an asset or the incurrence of a liability, as well as our ability to reasonably estimate the amount of loss in determining loss contingencies.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, particularly in the Software and Sensors segment, with evidence of customer willingness to pay more for subscriptions and services.

evidence.text: Our long-term financial strategy includes shifting our revenue, contracts, and cash flows from book-and-ship hardware transactions to multi-element, multi-year, subscription or recurring payment plans. During the three months ended September 30, 2018, 53% of our consolidated revenues were recognized from contracts with multiple performance obligations, while within our TASER Weapons and Software and Sensors segments, approximately 26% and 96%, respectively, were recognized from contracts containing multiple performance obligations. Recurring revenue refers to those contracts with multiple performance obligations, which we break out in more detail in the Critical Accounting Estimates.

factors.summary: The public safety technology market, particularly cloud-based software and sensors, is growing fastest, while hardware sales are decelerating.

evidence.text: The $5.8 billion cloud-based public safety software total addressable market estimates 2,100,000 public safety employees with annual records management & computer aided dispatch revenue reflecting an average of $100 per user per month [...] The hardware sensors $0.8 billion total addressable market estimates 660,000 domestic patrol officers and 1,000,000 immediately addressable international officers with annual camera, dock and other hardware sensors including Signal Sidearm with revenue of $30 per user per month based on our listed pricing, and 400,000 domestic patrol vehicles with annual hardware revenue of $52 per user per month based on 40% of allocation to hardware of our $129 per month listed Axon Fleet pricing.

factors.summary: Input costs are stable, with no significant changes in variable manufacturing costs.

evidence.text: Within the TASER Weapons segment, cost of product sales increased slightly to $19.3 million for the three months ended September 30, 2018 from $19.2 million for the same period in 2017. Cost as a percentage of sales decreased to 30.2% from 32.4% as a result of the sales mix, which resulted in increased revenue per unit.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Product and Service Sales

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, particularly in R&D, due to investments in new technologies and product development.

evidence.text: Research and development expense for the Software and Sensors segment increased $4.8 million, primarily due to a $2.6 million increase related to salaries and benefits, inclusive of stock-based compensation. We expect R&D expense to continue to increase in absolute dollars as we invest in the deployment of new CEW technologies and focus on growing the Software and Sensors segment as we add headcount and additional resources to develop new products and services to further advance our scalable cloud-connected device platform.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, particularly in the Software and Sensors segment, due to better matching of revenues and costs post-revenue recognition changes.

evidence.text: The increase in service margins during the three months ended September 30, 2018 as compared to the same period in 2017 was attributable to the reduction of non-recurring expenses related to our data migration to our new cloud-storage provider that was completed in 2018, as well as increased leveraging of fixed costs related to cloud-storage.

factors.summary: Supply chain health is stable, with no significant disruptions reported.

evidence.text: We noted no significant changes in variable manufacturing costs during the three months ended September 30, 2018 as compared to the same period in 2017.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Product and Service Sales

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing, particularly in the Software and Sensors segment, as the company scales up production to meet growing demand.

evidence.text: Our long-term financial strategy includes shifting our revenue, contracts, and cash flows from book-and-ship hardware transactions to multi-element, multi-year, subscription or recurring payment plans. [...] We are also highly focused on driving operating leverage and profitability within our two reportable segments, TASER Weapons and Software and Sensors.

factors.summary: Product quality incidents are minimal, with one notable customer incident leading to a transition to Axon technology, which is being managed.

evidence.text: In October 2018, a customer experienced a camera overheating incident on a VIEVU camera. As a result, we anticipate that this customer will transition to Axon technology sooner than previously expected. This may have an impact on our purchase commitment liability or on our inventory reserve during the quarter ending December 31, 2018; however, we cannot reasonably estimate a range of possible losses at this time.

factors.summary: Management is confident in the long-term strategic direction, as evidenced by increased R&D investment and focus on recurring subscription models.

evidence.text: Our long-term financial strategy includes shifting our revenue, contracts, and cash flows from book-and-ship hardware transactions to multi-element, multi-year, subscription or recurring payment plans. [...] We expect R&D expense to continue to increase in absolute dollars as we invest in the deployment of new CEW technologies and focus on growing the Software and Sensors segment as we add headcount and additional resources to develop new products and services to further advance our scalable cloud-connected device platform.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include potential impacts from a customer transition away from VIEVU cameras and municipal budget cycles affecting sales timing.

evidence.text: In October 2018, a customer experienced a camera overheating incident on a VIEVU camera. As a result, we anticipate that this customer will transition to Axon technology sooner than previously expected. This may have an impact on our purchase commitment liability or on our inventory reserve during the quarter ending December 31, 2018; however, we cannot reasonably estimate a range of possible losses at this time. [...] We have historically experienced higher net sales in our second and fourth quarters compared to other quarters in our fiscal year due primarily to municipal budget cycles.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear and well-resourced, focusing on recurring subscription models and expanding cloud-based software solutions.

evidence.text: Our long-term financial strategy includes shifting our revenue, contracts, and cash flows from book-and-ship hardware transactions to multi-element, multi-year, subscription or recurring payment plans. [...] We are also highly focused on driving operating leverage and profitability within our two reportable segments, TASER Weapons and Software and Sensors.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include municipal budget cycles affecting sales timing and potential impacts from geopolitical factors such as trade tensions.

evidence.text: We have historically experienced higher net sales in our second and fourth quarters compared to other quarters in our fiscal year due primarily to municipal budget cycles. [...] Are geopolitical factors (conflicts, sanctions, trade tensions, political instability) creating material risks, disruptions, or opportunities?

factors.summary: A specific near-term catalyst is the expected launch of TASER 7, which includes a software subscription with Axon Evidence, expected to drive substantial recurring payment plan subscriptions in 2019.

evidence.text: We expect recurring payment plan subscriptions to increase substantially in 2019 as we drive sales of TASER 7, which includes a software subscription with Axon Evidence.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes are not identified as having a significant impact on near-term results, though compliance costs could arise from environmental regulations.

evidence.text: Are regulatory changes creating new costs, restricting operations, or opening opportunities for the company? [...] environmental regulations and emissions requirements creating significant compliance costs or operational constraints? Or is the company ahead of requirements?

factors.summary: The company is adopting digital technologies and automation, particularly in cloud-based services, which is driving efficiency and competitive advantage.

evidence.text: We have also developed a fully integrated hardware and cloud-based software solution to provide our law enforcement customers the capabilities to capture, securely store, manage, share and analyze video and other digital evidence. [...] The $5.8 billion cloud-based public safety software total addressable market estimates 2,100,000 public safety employees with annual records management & computer aided dispatch revenue reflecting an average of $100 per user per month, based on a discount to estimated market pricing and analysis of current existing records management systems (''RMS'') and computer aided dispatch (''CAD'') contracts.

factors.summary: Newly launched products, such as Axon Fleet and Axon Evidence, are gaining traction with customers, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Additionally, we recorded a $0.7 million increase in revenue related to Axon Fleet, our in-car camera system that was released toward the end of 2017. [...] Axon Evidence and cloud services 23,915 [...].

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are expected to be in the range of $10 million to $12 million for the year ending December 31, 2018, signaling confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: We anticipate investing in capital expenditures in the range of $10 million to $12 million.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is stable, with no significant changes reported in the balance sheet.

evidence.text: We noted no significant changes in variable manufacturing costs during the three months ended September 30, 2018 as compared to the same period in 2017.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Product and Service Sales

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are focused on growth and efficiency rather than immediate returns, with no mention of buybacks or dividends in the provided text.

evidence.text: Our long-term financial strategy includes shifting our revenue, contracts, and cash flows from book-and-ship hardware transactions to multi-element, multi-year, subscription or recurring payment plans. [...] We are also highly focused on driving operating leverage and profitability within our two reportable segments, TASER Weapons and Software and Sensors.

factors.summary: The economic environment is favorable, with strong demand in key markets supporting growth.

evidence.text: For the year ending December 31, 2018, we expect revenue growth of between 18% and 20% as compared to the year ended December 31, 2017.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements have a minor impact, with some offsetting effects on revenue and costs.

evidence.text: During the three months ended September 30, 2018, interest and other income amounts consisted primarily of interest income related to our sales under hardware installment sale plans and investment and interest income totaling $1.7 million, which was partially offset by losses on foreign currency transaction adjustments of $0.4 million.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks are not a significant factor in the near term, though trade tensions could impact the supply chain.

evidence.text: Are geopolitical factors (conflicts, sanctions, trade tensions, political instability) creating material risks, disruptions, or opportunities?

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing across multiple product lines, driven by increased sales of TASER weapons and Axon products, as well as higher demand for cloud services and subscriptions.

evidence.text: Cash used in operations was also impacted by increased accounts and notes receivable of $26.0 million, of which $20.4 million related to increased customer balances under our Officer Safety Plan and TASER 60 purchase program while the remaining increase was attributable to increased trade receivable balances resulting from higher net sales.
Net unit sales for TASER Weapons handles and other products and Software and Sensors segment products were as follows [...] Axon Evidence and cloud services 23,915 [...]

factors.summary: The order backlog is growing, as evidenced by both increased deferred revenue and higher bookings in the Software and Sensors segment. Cash flows from operations have been positively impacted by this growth, with deferred revenue increasing by $31.7 million. Additionally, new orders have outpaced revenue, further indicating a strengthening backlog.

evidence.text: Increases to operating cash flows consisted primarily of increased deferred revenue of $31.7 million and decreased inventory of $9.0 million. The increase in deferred revenue was primarily driven by increased Software and Sensors services invoiced in advance.

Bookings related to our Software and Sensors segment, net of cancellations, were $92.9 million and $78.0 million during the three months ended September 30, 2018 and 2017, respectively, an increase of $14.9 million, or 19.1%. Most bookings will be invoiced in subsequent periods.

factors.summary: The company is managing its inventory levels, with provisions made for excess and obsolete inventory. However, there is no significant risk of obsolescence or shortage as reported.

evidence.text: During the nine months ended September 30, 2018 and 2017, we recorded provisions for excess and obsolete inventory of $2.8 million and $1.5 million, respectively.
We noted no significant changes in variable manufacturing costs during the three months ended September 30, 2018 as compared to the same period in 2017.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Product and Service Sales

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is positive and accelerating across both the TASER Weapons and Software and Sensors segments. The evidence highlights strong performance in both segments, with significant year-over-year increases in net sales, contributing to the overall growth.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by (used in) operating activities in the first nine months of 2018 of $32.6 million reflects $27.1 million in net income impacted by the net increase of non-cash income statement items totaling $23.7 million and cash outflows of $18.2 million for the net change in operating assets and liabilities.

Net sales for the TASER Weapons segment increased 10.4% primarily due to increased sales under the Officer Safety Plan and TASER 60 payment programs. Net sales for the Software and Sensors segment increased 48.6%...
For the year ending December 31, 2018, we expect revenue growth of between 18% and 20% as compared to the year ended December 31, 2017.

Net sales for the Software and Sensors segment increased 33.5% as we continued to add users to our network during the three months ended September 30, 2018, resulting in steady product revenues and a higher number of aggregate users, which resulted in increased Axon Evidence and extended warranty revenues of $7.7 million and $1.2 million, respectively.

factors.summary: Gross margins are expanding, driven by increased leveraging of fixed costs and improved efficiency. While gross margins are under pressure due to increased costs, operating margins may be stable or improving due to cost management.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased $49.2 million to $193.2 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2018 compared to $144.1 million for the same period in 2017. As a percentage of net sales, gross margin increased to 63.3% for the nine months ended September 30, 2018 compared to 57.8% for the same period in 2017...

Gross margin increased $15.9 million to $65.6 million for the three months ended September 30, 2018 compared to $49.8 million for the same period in 2017. As a percentage of net sales, gross margin increased to 62.6% for the three months ended September 30, 2018 compared to 55.1% for the same period in 2017, which was primarily attributable to increased leveraging of fixed costs related to cloud storage.

Net cash generated by financing activities was $222.2 million during the first nine months of 2018. In May 2018, we completed a public follow-on equity offering that generated net proceeds of $234.0 million which was partially offset by income and payroll taxes of $12.0 million paid by us on behalf of employees who net-settled stock awards during the period.

factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating, particularly in international markets and within the Software and Sensors segment.

evidence.text: International revenue grew 34.4%, driven by strength in Australia, Canada, and the U.K.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2018 Compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2017

evidence.text: Net sales for the Software and Sensors segment increased 33.5% as we continued to add users to our network during the three months ended September 30, 2018, resulting in steady product revenues and a higher number of aggregate users, which resulted in increased Axon Evidence and extended warranty revenues of $7.7 million and $1.2 million, respectively. Additionally, we recorded a $0.7 million increase in revenue related to Axon Fleet, our in-car camera system that was released toward the end of 2017.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting positively towards higher-margin segments, particularly the Software and Sensors segment, which has seen significant growth and margin improvements. This shift is driven by increased adoption of products like on-officer cameras and Axon Evidence software suite, as well as leveraging of fixed costs related to cloud storage.

evidence.text: Net sales for the Software and Sensors segment increased 48.6%, primarily due to continued adoption of on-officer cameras and related technologies, including our Axon Evidence digital evidence management software suite.

Gross margin increased $15.9 million to $65.6 million for the three months ended September 30, 2018 compared to $49.8 million for the same period in 2017. As a percentage of net sales, gross margin increased to 62.6% for the three months ended September 30, 2018 compared to 55.1% for the same period in 2017, which was primarily attributable to increased leveraging of fixed costs related to cloud storage.

factors.summary: Cost actions have been successful, with cost of product and service sales increasing moderately but as a percentage of net sales decreasing. Additionally, cost reduction initiatives, such as data migration to a new cloud-storage provider, have delivered measurable savings.

evidence.text: Cost of product and service sales was $112.0 million and $105.1 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2018 and 2017, respectively, an increase of $6.9 million, or 6.6%. As a percentage of net sales, cost of product and service sales decreased to 36.7% for the nine months ended September 30, 2018 compared to 42.2% during the same period in 2017.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Product and Service Sales

evidence.text: Within the Software and Sensors segment, cost of product and service sales decreased to $19.9 million for the three months ended September 30, 2018 from $21.3 million for the same period in 2017 primarily due to the reduction of non-recurring expenses related to our data migration to our new cloud-storage provider that was completed in 2018, as well as increased leveraging of fixed costs related to cloud-storage.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Product and Service Sales

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, supported by increased cash on hand and positive cash flow from operations, as well as higher gross margins and operating efficiency. This improvement is reflected in the significant increase in cash and cash equivalents, and the notable rise in net income.

evidence.text: As of September 30, 2018, we had $326.8 million of cash, cash equivalents and restricted cash, an increase of $248.4 million as compared to December 31, 2017...

Gross margin increased $15.9 million to $65.6 million for the three months ended September 30, 2018 compared to $49.8 million for the same period in 2017. [...] Net income increased by $5.3 million to $5.7 million for the three months ended September 30, 2018 compared to $0.4 million for the same period in 2017.

factors.summary: The company is significantly increasing its R&D investment, particularly in the Software and Sensors segment, driven by the need to develop new technologies, products, and services. The increase is substantial, with a 40.3% year-over-year growth in R&D expenses, primarily attributed to investments in new technologies and product development.

evidence.text: R&D expenses were comprised as follows (dollars in thousands): Total research and development expenses $55,602 for 2018 compared to $39,618 for 2017, an increase of $15,984 or 40.3%. The increase was primarily driven by investments in new technologies and product development.

Research and development expense for the Software and Sensors segment increased $4.8 million, primarily due to a $2.6 million increase related to salaries and benefits, inclusive of stock-based compensation. We expect R&D expense to continue to increase in absolute dollars as we invest in the deployment of new CEW technologies and focus on growing the Software and Sensors segment as we add headcount and additional resources to develop new products and services to further advance our scalable cloud-connected device platform.

factors.summary: Management has maintained forward guidance for revenue growth, indicating confidence in continued growth.

evidence.text: Management has not explicitly raised or lowered forward guidance, but the strong performance suggests confidence in future growth.
For the year ending December 31, 2018, we expect revenue growth of between 18% and 20% as compared to the year ended December 31, 2017.

factors.summary: The company has not reported any recent mergers or acquisitions that would impact its business. The evidence highlights a strategic shift towards subscription-based revenue models rather than traditional hardware transactions.

evidence.text: No specific M&A activity was discussed in the provided text.

Our long-term financial strategy includes shifting our revenue, contracts, and cash flows from book-and-ship hardware transactions to multi-element, multi-year, subscription or recurring payment plans.

factors.summary: The impact of interest rate changes on the company's tax situation is mixed. While discrete tax benefits have favorably affected the effective tax rate, the overall impact of interest rates on borrowing costs remains manageable but partially offsets potential increases.

evidence.text: The provision for income taxes was a benefit of $2.0 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2018, which was an effective tax rate of (8.1)%. This was partially offset by interest rate impacts on borrowings...

Our estimated full year effective income tax rate for 2018, before discrete period adjustments, is 24.0% [...] This was partially offset by R&D tax credit deductions. The effective tax rate was favorably impacted by a $2.0 million discrete tax benefit primarily associated with windfalls related to stock-based compensation for RSUs that vested or stock options that were exercised during the three months ended September 30, 2018.
